improvements: Replace the brittle `.card` CSS selector with a broader candidate selector list in the `resolveLocator` fallback chain — add alternatives such as `[data-testid='product-card']`, `[class*='product']`, `[class*='item']`, `.product-item`, `.product-list > *`, `li.product`, and `article` so the framework can self-heal when the actual DOM uses a different class name than `.card`

Instrument the test to perform a DOM snapshot (page.content() or page.evaluate) after navigation and log all top-level container class names to stdout before the assertion step, enabling faster diagnosis of the real selector without needing a trace viewer

Add an explicit `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` or `waitForSelector` with a generous timeout (e.g., 15 s) before attempting to resolve the product list locator, ruling out timing/race conditions as a contributing factor on slower CI environments

Reduce screenshot file size before sending to the validator: compress the PNG (e.g., use `page.screenshot({ type: 'jpeg', quality: 60 })`) or clip to the viewport center to stay under the 5 MB API limit and unblock automated visual validation

Introduce a data-testid attribute (`data-testid='product-card'`) on the product card component in the application source and update the selector strategy to target it as the primary locator, making tests resilient to CSS refactoring

Add a smoke-level pre-check that asserts the API endpoint (e.g., GET /api/products) returns HTTP 200 and a non-empty JSON array before the UI assertion; if the API fails, the test should be marked as blocked rather than failed to distinguish app bugs from selector issues

Create a shared `ProductListPage` POM class that encapsulates all product-list selectors and exposes a `waitForProducts()` method, preventing selector duplication across future test cases for this story

Capture a full-page screenshot at a reduced resolution or as a clipped region (top 800 px) instead of the full page to ensure validator image payloads stay within the 5 MB threshold on all viewport sizes

cases.validationSummary: The landing page loads successfully and displays 6 product cards with visible, legible product names, satisfying all validation criteria.
improvements: Replace the hardcoded '.card' selector with a broader candidate list in the resolveLocator call: try '[class*="card"]', '[data-testid*="product"]', '.product-item', 'article', and '[class*="product"]' as fallback candidates so the framework's firstExisting() resolves the actual DOM structure instead of failing on a single class name

Add a pre-assertion page-load guard using 'await page.waitForLoadState("networkidle")' and 'await page.waitForSelector("h2, [class*=card], article", { timeout: 10000 })' before any product card locator resolution to ensure the product grid is fully rendered before assertions run

Update the product card locator strategy to use a semantic or data-attribute selector such as '[data-testid="product-card"]' or the actual rendered element structure (e.g., '.product-grid > *' or 'ul.products > li') discovered from a DOM inspection of the live page, since screenshots confirm 6 cards render correctly but the '.card' class does not match the actual class names in the DOM

Introduce a locator discovery step in the test scaffold that logs all candidate matches for product-like elements (article, section > div, [class*=card], [class*=product]) when no primary selector resolves, enabling faster diagnosis in future failures without requiring trace inspection

Refactor the product name assertion to iterate over resolved product card locators and check 'innerText' of the child heading element using a relative locator (e.g., locator.locator('h2, h3, [class*=name], [class*=title]').first()) so the test remains resilient to heading level changes

Add a minimum product count assertion (expect(productCards.count()).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3)) after resolving the product list to formally enforce the 'multiple products' acceptance criterion that the manual case and validation both confirm is met

Capture the actual DOM class names of product cards during a successful navigation using 'page.evaluate(() => [...document.querySelectorAll("main *")].map(el => el.className))' in a debug/discovery test run, then hardcode the confirmed selector into the POM to eliminate guesswork across all product-related test cases
